reasoning: The Lean statement uses `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` to encode $\mathbb{R}^k$, takes `hE : ¬ Set.Countable E` as the uncountability hypothesis, and defines P as `{x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}`, which is exactly the definition of condensation points (every neighborhood of x meets E in an uncountable set; note that 𝓝 x in Mathlib corresponds to the standard neighborhood filter). The conclusion `Set.Countable (E \ P)` precisely captures "at most countably many points of E are not in P". No extra or missing hypotheses, and the types match. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
